rubric: Names at least 4 AI code review tools relevant in 2026.
Explicitly includes GitHub Copilot code review or another GitHub-native code review offering.
Explicitly includes GitLab Duo Code Review or Atlassian Bitbucket AI as a non-GitHub platform-native alternative.
States the primary role or workflow fit of GitHub Copilot code review.
States the primary role or workflow fit of CodeRabbit.
States the primary role or workflow fit of Amazon CodeGuru Reviewer or an AWS-native review tool.
States the primary role or workflow fit of Qodo Merge or an equivalent dedicated AI PR reviewer.
Provides a pricing or deployment stance for at least 2 tools, such as free tier, SaaS, enterprise-only, or self-hosted.
Mentions at least 2 integration surfaces, such as GitHub pull requests, CI, IDE, GitLab MRs, or Gerrit.
States at least 1 concrete limitation for at least 2 tools.
Explicitly distinguishes AI code review from static analysis or security scanning.
Mentions at least 1 open-source or self-hosted code review option.
Explicitly anchors the landscape in 2026 or uses equivalent wording such as "as of 2026."
Compares at least 3 tools head-to-head on workflow fit or review style.
States at least 1 tradeoff between GitHub-native review assistants and standalone reviewer bots.
Recommends different tools for at least 2 different buyer profiles or team contexts.
Explains at least 1 reason teams adopt dedicated AI reviewers in addition to IDE copilots.
Identifies at least 1 directional trend across 2025-2026 and explains why it happened.
Identifies at least 1 unresolved risk or open problem in AI code review.
Segments the market into at least 3 categories or archetypes.
Supports every major tool mention with at least 1 citation.
Includes an explicit gap declaration or uncertainty section.
Uses distinct labeled sections for comparison and recommendations.
